definition: A departure from what is normal or expected, typically an unwelcome one
exampleSentence: The unusually cold summer was dismissed as a statistical aberration rather than evidence of climate change.

definition: To repeal or do away with a law, right, or formal agreement
exampleSentence: The government moved to abrogate the outdated trade agreement that had been in place for decades.

definition: Indulging only very moderately in something, especially food and drink
exampleSentence: His abstemious lifestyle, avoiding alcohol and rich foods, contributed to his remarkable longevity.

definition: Angry and bitter in tone or manner
exampleSentence: The acrimonious debate between the two candidates revealed deep ideological divisions within the party.

definition: The ability to make good judgments and quick decisions in a particular domain
exampleSentence: Her business acumen was evident in the way she transformed the struggling startup into a profitable enterprise.

definition: To warn or reprimand someone firmly but not harshly
exampleSentence: The judge admonished the defendant for their reckless behavior before handing down the sentence.

definition: Concerned with beauty or the appreciation of beauty
exampleSentence: The architect prioritized both the functional and aesthetic aspects of the building's design.

definition: To increase the power, status, or wealth of
exampleSentence: Critics accused the leader of using public funds to aggrandize himself and his close associates.
She accepted the challenging new assignment with alacrity, eager to prove her capabilities.

definition: To make a bad or unpleasant situation better
exampleSentence: The government introduced several measures to ameliorate the living conditions in overcrowded urban areas.

definition: Something belonging to a period other than the one in which it exists
exampleSentence: The monarchy is viewed by many as an anachronism in modern democratic society.

definition: Something or someone that one vehemently dislikes
exampleSentence: The concept of censorship is anathema to those who champion freedom of expression.

definition: A deep-seated feeling of dislike or aversion
exampleSentence: There has always been a strong antipathy between the two rival factions within the organization.

definition: Of doubtful authenticity, although widely circulated as being true
exampleSentence: The story of the CEO starting the company in a garage turned out to be largely apocryphal.
The researcher's groundbreaking findings earned widespread approbation from the scientific community.

definition: Based on random choice or personal whim, rather than any reason or system
exampleSentence: The selection criteria appeared arbitrary and lacked any objective justification.

definition: Involving or requiring strenuous effort; difficult and tiring
exampleSentence: The arduous journey through the mountains tested the endurance of even the most experienced climbers.

definition: Characterized by severe self-discipline and abstention from indulgence
exampleSentence: The monk's ascetic lifestyle, devoid of material possessions, was admired by some and questioned by others.

definition: Showing great care, attention, and persistent effort
exampleSentence: Her assiduous preparation for the examination paid off when she achieved the highest score in her cohort.
